decision: Do not spend the next loop proving more missing rows. Use the accepted regime factor consumer map now, and reopen missing species/full-matrix gaps only through source-owned positive rows plus matched negatives or owner-approved exact source-label extensions.

consumed_artifacts.use: fail-closed intake shape for spoofing/layering rows
negative readiness evidence: positive cases exist, matched negatives absent

web_research_decisions.decision: Use as modeling/backtesting inspiration for HMM posterior, persistence, and temporal isolation only; do not treat inferred hidden states as source labels.
Use only as an HMM implementation reference where repo code already expects HMM-style posterior fields; not a label source.
Use for change-point boundary recency/intensity features; not a tradable regime label by itself.
Use its order-flow/spoofability feature framing for future direct-order-flow probes; not a source-owned row export.
Use as a detector/probe reference only. It emits heuristic manipulation detections and synthetic examples, so it cannot supply accepted labels or matched controls.
Potential acquisition path for order-lifecycle rows and matched negative controls if an approved account/API export is available; it is not, by itself, positive spoofing/layering labels.

stop_conditions: Do not rerun broad OHLCV/proxy/source sweeps for Manipulation.
Do not call spoofing/layering absence a failure of the accepted scoped Manipulation overlay.
Do not promote HMM, change-point, provider bars, or detector outputs as labels.
next_action: Hand off docs/experiments/actionable-regime-confidence/runs/20260511T153637-codex-regime-factor-consumer-map-v1/regime-factor-map/regime_factor_consumer_map_v1.csv as the consumer contract; open a new Board A loop only for source-owned direct rows with matched negatives or an approved exact source-label panel extension.
